sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Runway Growth Finance Launches Unsecured Notes Offering
feed.summary: Runway Growth Finance (RWAY) has initiated an underwritten offering of unsecured notes to be listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market. The net proceeds will primarily be used to repay existing debt, including $51.75 million of 8.00% notes due in December 2027, and to support the prior acquisition of SWK Holdings, which will boost its healthcare investment portfolio to 31%. Despite a 14% dividend yield and a 30% discount to NAV, analysts maintain a cautious outlook on RWAY's future performance, with an average 1-year price target of $10.57.

feed.title: Radware buys Pynt to bolster pre-production API security
feed.summary: Radware has acquired API security testing specialist Pynt to enhance its API security product line with pre-production testing capabilities. This acquisition aims to close the gap between shift-left and shift-right strategies, offering a unified platform for API discovery, posture management, business logic protection, runtime defense, and testing. Pynt's technology will continue to be available as a standalone offering while also being integrated into Radware's broader security portfolio.

feed.title: Deere to Move Excavator Production From Japan to North Carolina
feed.summary: Deere & Co. plans to relocate its excavator production from Japan to a new $70 million facility in Kernersville, North Carolina, and establish a distribution center in Hebron, Indiana. This strategic move aims to strengthen American manufacturing, creating approximately 150 jobs at each new location. The company announced these plans in a statement on Tuesday.

feed.title: Northern Trust (NTRS) COO Cherecwich sells $3.74 million in stock
feed.summary: Northern Trust's Chief Operating Officer, Peter Cherecwich, sold 25,000 shares of company stock for $3.74 million on January 26, 2026. Following the sale, Cherecwich still holds significant direct and indirect ownership in NTRS. This transaction comes after the company reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations, leading to several price target increases from financial analysts.

feed.title: Mizuho upgrades Applied Materials stock to Outperform on WFE growth
feed.summary: Mizuho has upgraded Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) from Neutral to Outperform, raising its price target to $370 from $275, citing accelerating capital expenditure in the US, Taiwan, and Japan. The firm projects significant WFE growth for 2026 and 2027, driven by increased spending from TSMC and Intel. Applied Materials' strong financial health, impressive returns, and diversified revenue streams, particularly outside China, contributed to the bullish outlook.

feed.title: Anterix (NASDAQ:ATEX) Shares Up 15% - Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Anterix (NASDAQ:ATEX) saw its shares jump 15% on Tuesday on higher-than-average trading volume. The company has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average price target of $50, despite mixed individual ratings including an "overweight" from JPMorgan and a "strong-buy" from Zacks. Insider and institutional ownership is substantial, and the company recently beat quarterly EPS expectations.

feed.title: Trustmark Corp (NASDAQ:TRMK) Q4 2025 Earnings Beat Estimates Despite Revenue Miss
feed.summary: Trustmark Corporation reported Q4 2025 earnings per share of $0.97, surpassing analyst estimates of $0.93, despite revenue of $207.1 million falling short of the $210.2 million forecast. The company achieved record net income and total revenue for the full fiscal year 2025, with solid balance sheet growth, robust net interest margin, and an increased quarterly dividend. The market reaction to the mixed results was muted, with the stock showing little movement in after-hours trading.

feed.title: WATCH: How AI helps Republic Services keep workers safe, recycle efficiently
feed.summary: Republic Services has implemented AI technology in its Mesa recycling center to enhance efficiency and worker safety. Three AI robots have recovered 4.5 million tons of recyclables, reducing landfill waste and CO2 emissions. Additionally, AI on forklifts detects objects and people, stopping the vehicle to prevent accidents, showcasing the company's investment in advanced technology for both environmental and safety benefits.

feed.title: F5 shares jump after revenue and earnings beat in fiscal first quarter
feed.summary: F5 Inc. experienced a significant jump in shares after reporting strong revenue and earnings that surpassed analyst expectations in its fiscal first quarter. The application security firm reported adjusted EPS of $4.45 and revenue of $822 million, driven by strong systems revenue growth. The company also raised its full-year outlook, reflecting confidence in continued demand for its hybrid multicloud architectures and AI-driven solutions.

feed.title: PACCAR Financial, Parts rise despite softer truck demand
feed.summary: PACCAR reported solid financial results for 2025, with strong performance from its parts and financial services divisions mitigating a decline in truck deliveries and earnings. PACCAR Financial Services saw a 5% increase in revenue and an 11% rise in pretax income, while PACCAR Parts also achieved record annual revenue. The company expects improving freight fundamentals and continued investment in technology to support a stronger truck market in 2026.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Declares AI Essential: Radiology's Staffing Crisis Forces Workflow Revolution
feed.summary: GE HealthCare has declared artificial intelligence as essential for radiology departments, not a luxury, due to severe staffing shortages and increasing demand. AI tools are being utilized to optimize workflows, reduce clinician burnout, and boost capacity by leveraging previously unused healthcare data. The company is actively acquiring related technologies and forming partnerships to accelerate AI deployment across healthcare settings, addressing a critical workforce crisis in radiology.

feed.title: American Electric Power Recognized as Low-Risk Retirement Stock
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) is highlighted as a low-risk retirement stock due to a recent price target increase by Barclays, strong investor interest from industry experts like Sean Russo, and a substantial $72 billion five-year capital investment plan. The company's attractive dividend yield of approximately 3.26% further enhances its appeal for stable returns. AEP's strategic focus on meeting increased power demand from data centers and manufacturing through its capital plan positions it for future growth and investor confidence.

feed.title: Packaging Corp Missed Estimates As Big Customers Ordered Less
feed.summary: Packaging Corp of America reported weaker-than-expected fourth-quarter results, missing both profit and sales forecasts due to softer demand for corrugated boxes from major consumer brands and higher operating costs. Management attributed the decreased orders to inflation and economic uncertainty, leading customers to keep inventory levels lean. The company also faced disruptions from a winter storm and anticipates significant charges and job cuts related to closing a Washington facility, highlighting the increasing importance of resilience for heavy-asset manufacturers.

feed.title: Totino’s Skips The Super Bowl To Expand Fan Universe Around Chazmo, The Loveable Alien
feed.summary: Totino's is forgoing a Super Bowl advertisement to instead launch a digital musical featuring its popular alien mascot, Chazmo. This strategic shift is driven by overwhelming fan engagement and online views exceeding 10 million for videos featuring the character. The brand aims to treat Chazmo as expandable intellectual property, creating longer-form entertainment to meet audience demand and compete in a market where brands are increasingly compared to entertainment experiences.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: F5 Networks Q1 2026 beats earnings expectations
feed.summary: F5 Networks reported strong Q1 2026 earnings, surpassing EPS and revenue forecasts, which led to a 1.11% rise in its stock during aftermarket trading. The company's performance was driven by significant growth in systems revenue and increased demand from hybrid multi-cloud adoption, AI investments, and converged platforms. F5 Networks has also raised its revenue growth outlook for FY2026 and plans strategic investments for future growth.

feed.title: John Deere brings over 150 NC factory jobs, 150 more in Indiana
feed.summary: John Deere announced plans for two new U.S. facilities: a parts distribution center in Hebron, Indiana, and a $70 million excavator factory in Kernersville, North Carolina. These expansions are expected to create approximately 150 jobs at each location, with the North Carolina factory taking over excavator production from Japan. This move reinforces John Deere's commitment to investing $20 billion in U.S. manufacturing over the next decade.

feed.title: Delta Was Not Done After This Month’s Boeing Order — Now It’s Buying Airbus A330s And A350s Too
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has announced a new order for 16 Airbus A330-900s and 15 Airbus A350-900s, with options for 20 more widebody aircraft. This follows a recent order for Boeing 787-10s, indicating Delta's continued expansion of its fleet. The new Airbus planes will help fill delivery gaps and provide greater range for long-haul routes, with deliveries starting in 2029.

feed.title: RTX CEO backs dividend after Trump attacks on weapons makers
feed.summary: The CEO of RTX, a major defense contractor, has affirmed the company's commitment to its dividend strategy despite recent political rhetoric. This statement comes after former President Donald Trump criticized defense manufacturers, an action that could raise concerns among investors regarding the stability of returns from such companies. By backing the dividend, RTX aims to reassure shareholders and maintain investor confidence in its financial performance.

feed.title: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) Releases Earnings Results, Misses Expectations By $0.09 EPS
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) reported quarterly earnings per share of $2.32, missing analysts' consensus estimates by $0.09. Despite the earnings miss, PKG stock traded up 0.7% to $223.28, with higher-than-average trading volume. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $1.25, reflecting an annualized dividend of $5.00 and a 2.2% yield.

feed.title: Trump notes $70 million factory investment by tractor maker Deere in North Carolina
feed.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that farm-equipment maker Deere & Co (DE.N) is making a $70 million factory investment in North Carolina. This investment was previously announced in 2024 by the company, when former President Joe Biden was in office, and is expected to create over 150 new jobs in Kernersville.

feed.title: Moody’s downgrades Gogo’s rating to B2 amid rising competition
feed.summary: Moody's has downgraded Gogo Inc.'s corporate family rating to B2 from B1, while maintaining a stable outlook. This decision reflects intensifying competition in Gogo's business jet connectivity market and execution risks as the company shifts its global strategy. The rating agency also cited high debt leverage and limited visibility into Gogo's deleveraging pace.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger’s senior unsecured rating upgraded to A1 by Moody’s
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has upgraded W.W. Grainger, Inc.'s senior unsecured rating to A1 from A2, maintaining a stable outlook, citing the company's strong cash generation, conservative financial leverage, and leading position in the industrial distribution sector. The upgrade reflects Grainger's ability to manage macroeconomic uncertainties and inflationary pressures through its significant scale, diverse customer base, and strategic investments in technology. Future upgrades would require greater revenue scale with reduced cyclical market exposure, while maintaining strong credit metrics.

feed.title: W.W. Grainger’s senior unsecured rating upgraded to A1 by Moody’s
feed.summary: Moody’s Ratings has upgraded W.W. Grainger, Inc.'s senior unsecured rating to A1 from A2, maintaining a stable outlook. The upgrade reflects Grainger's strong cash generation, conservative financial leverage, significant scale in industrial distribution, and effective management of inflationary pressures. The company's market position, diverse customer base, and investments in technology are expected to drive continued strong credit metrics.

feed.title: Vale Beats 2025 Production Targets as Iron Ore and Copper Hit Highest Levels Since 2018
feed.summary: Vale SA (VALE) reported strong operational results for 2025, exceeding its production guidance across all segments, with iron ore and copper output reaching their highest levels since 2018. The company's fourth-quarter performance showed increased volumes in iron ore, copper, and nickel, along with a strategic shift in its product mix to optimize value amidst weaker pellet premiums. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates VALE as Outperform, noting its attractive valuation, strong earnings, and solid financials, despite some concerns about modest revenue growth and technical overbought conditions.

feed.title: Prologis Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividends
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc. has announced the tax treatment for its 2025 distributions for both common and preferred stock, providing detailed exhibits as prescribed by the Internal Revenue Code. Shareholders will receive IRS Form 1099-DIV via Computershare or their brokerage firm. The company advises consulting a tax advisor as federal, state, and local tax laws vary and affect taxpayers differently.

feed.title: Arbor Realty's Huge 16% Yield: Don't Fold Yet (NYSE:ABR)
feed.summary: Arbor Realty Trust (ABR) offers a high 16% dividend yield, but it carries a significant risk of further dividend cuts due to past reliance on one-time gains and investor concerns over credit quality. Despite trading at a low 0.64x book value, ABR could see some relief with falling interest rates in 2026, which may benefit its loan portfolio. The article suggests that while high-risk, ABR's low valuation, insider buying, and potential for a decent yield even after a cut might appeal to enterprising investors.

feed.title: John Deere Announces Major Expansion with Two New U.S. Facilities Coming
feed.summary: John Deere is expanding its U.S. operations with two new facilities: a distribution center near Hebron, Indiana, and an excavator factory in Kernersville, North Carolina. These investments aim to strengthen the American industry, create hundreds of new jobs, and enhance John Deere's supply chain and manufacturing capabilities. The company plans to invest $20 billion in U.S. manufacturing over the next decade.

feed.title: Packaging Corp.: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Packaging Corp. of America (PKG) reported fourth-quarter earnings of $101.8 million, or $1.13 per share, adjusted to $2.32 per share, falling short of Wall Street estimates. The company's revenue of $2.36 billion also missed analyst expectations. For the full year, the company posted a profit of $774.1 million on revenue of $8.99 billion.

feed.title: News | Raymond James expands New York footprint at Park Avenue office tower
feed.summary: Investment bank Raymond James is expanding its New York office presence at 320 Park Avenue by subleasing more than 38,000 square feet from Flagstar. This move consolidates its footprint in the corporate-headquarters-heavy corridor, which is known for a tight vacancy rate and high demand from financial services firms. This expansion follows Raymond James's fifth consecutive year of record annual results and strengthens its position as the largest tenant in the Munich Re-owned tower.

feed.title: CF Industries (CF) Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.50/Share
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (CF) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.50 per share, resulting in a forward yield of 2.18%. The company, a leading producer of nitrogen fertilizers, is also involved in strategic initiatives such as a carbon capture project with Exxon. Despite an Altman Z-Score indicating some financial stress, CF Industries demonstrates robust financial health and a strong market position.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Raises Dividend by 4.4%
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has announced a 4.4% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.8875 per share, bringing its forward dividend yield to 3.37%. This move reflects the company's dedication to shareholder value within a regulated utilities sector, despite facing challenges like a declining gross margin and moderate leverage. The stock is currently trading within its historical valuation ranges, with technical indicators suggesting it's approaching overbought territory.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Announces Increased Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has declared an increased quarterly dividend of 88.75 cents per share, payable on March 16 to shareholders as of February 18. This represents an annual increase of 15 cents, continuing the company's commitment to shareholder value. Despite a decline in operating margin over the past five years and its Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial distress, the regulated utility maintains a significant market position and a low volatility typical of its sector.

feed.title: Chevron appoints veteran executive Thomas Horton to board
feed.summary: Chevron has appointed former American Airlines Chairman and CEO Thomas W. Horton as an independent director to its board, where he will also serve on the audit committee. Horton, 64, is a partner at Global Infrastructure Partners and holds board positions at major companies like Walmart and GE Aerospace. This appointment comes as Chevron CEO Mike Wirth is reportedly discussing his own departure from the company with the board of directors.

feed.title: Busey (BUSE) Surpasses Q4 Revenue Estimates and Strengthens Fina
feed.summary: Busey (BUSE) exceeded its Q4 revenue estimates, reporting $200.2 million, and demonstrated strong financial health with improved return on average assets and net interest margin. The company saw record wealth management fees, driven by a 4.7% increase in assets under care, and maintained a solid capital structure. Despite a decrease in deposits due to strategic fund reduction, Busey appears well-prepared for varying economic conditions in 2026.

feed.title: US firm Applied Materials opens 8.06 lakh sq.ft office in City
feed.summary: Applied Materials India, a subsidiary of Applied Materials, Inc., inaugurated an 8.06 lakh square-foot workplace at the International Tech Park Bengaluru (ITPL). This expansion is expected to strengthen Karnataka's position as a leading hub for Electronics System Design & Manufacturing (ESDM) and a chip design cluster in India. The new facility, along with existing capabilities like the India Validation Centre and an AI Centre of Excellence in Chennai, underscores Applied Materials' commitment to enhancing India's semiconductor ecosystem.

feed.title: Insider Sale: Chief Accounting Officer of $SLB Sells 13,268 Shares
feed.summary: Howard Guild, Chief Accounting Officer of SLB (NYSE: SLB), sold 13,268 shares for an estimated $659,419 on January 26, 2026, representing about 37.2% of his holdings. This sale is part of a trend of 7 insider sales and no purchases at SLB over the last six months. The article also details recent hedge fund activity, Congressional trading, and analyst ratings for SLB.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp announces all-stock Field & Main merger
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) announced an all-stock merger agreement to acquire Field & Main Bancorp for approximately $105.7 million. This acquisition, expected to close in Q2 2026, will expand Stock Yards' presence in Western Kentucky and Indiana, creating a $10.4 billion-asset franchise. The deal is projected to be 5.7% accretive to earnings per share with minimal capital dilution, and Field & Main's CEO will join Stock Yards' board.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp to acquire Field & Main in $105.7 million deal
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp announced its acquisition of Field & Main Bancorp in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $105.7 million. The acquisition is expected to close in Q2 2026, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals, and will expand Stock Yards' presence in Western Kentucky. The combined entity will manage approximately $10.4 billion in assets, and the deal is projected to be 5.7% accretive to Stock Yards' earnings per share.

feed.title: CF Industries declares $0.50 per share dividend
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 13, 2026. The company will also release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on February 18, 2026, followed by a conference call on February 19, 2026. This announcement follows an optimistic outlook from analysts and recent positive developments including exceeding Q3 2025 earnings expectations and advancing low-carbon initiatives.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals raises quarterly dividend by 1.1% to $1.81/share
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (APD) has announced a 1.1% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $1.79 to $1.81 per share. This marks the 42nd consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, demonstrating a long-standing commitment to returning value to shareholders. The dividend is payable on May 13, 2025, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2025.

feed.title: Con Edison declares quarterly dividend, extends utility record
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of 88.75 cents per share, an increase of 15 cents annually, payable on March 16, 2026. This marks the company's 52nd consecutive annual dividend increase, the longest streak among S&P 500 utilities. The move reflects Con Edison's commitment to investor returns and reliable service during the clean energy transition, with the company targeting a dividend payout ratio between 55% and 65% of adjusted earnings.

feed.title: Vale's iron ore production rises 3% in 2025, surpassing Rio Tinto's Pilbara
feed.summary: Brazilian miner Vale's iron ore production reached 336.1 million metric tons in 2025, marking a 2.6% increase from 2024 and surpassing Rio Tinto's Pilbara operations for the first time since 2018. Vale's output in the fourth quarter of 2025 was 90.4 million tons, up 6% year-on-year. The company aims to regain its position as the world's largest iron ore producer after losing the title in 2019 following a dam collapse.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals Raises Quarterly Dividend to $1.81 a Share From $1.79, Payable May 11 to Holders of Record April 1
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE: APD) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $1.81 per share, up from the previous $1.79. This dividend is scheduled to be payable on May 11 to shareholders who are on record as of April 1.

feed.title: Woodside Energy posts record 2025 production as project pipeline advances
feed.summary: Woodside Energy Group achieved record oil and gas production in 2025, exceeding guidance, and maintained strong reliability across its key assets. The company also made significant progress on major projects like Scarborough and Trion, and strengthened strategic partnerships. Despite a flat share price over the past year, Woodside is focused on future production targets and project delivery.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) reported quarterly earnings of $1.24 EPS, exceeding analyst estimates by $0.04. The bank also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.32, translating to an annualized yield of 1.9%. Analysts currently rate the stock as "Hold" with an average target price of $83.67.

feed.title: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) Trading Up 2.2% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) saw its shares rise by 2.2% after Evercore ISI upgraded the stock to "outperform" and increased its price target to $100, citing AI-driven networking demand and the new "Cisco 360" partner program. Several other analysts also raised their ratings and price targets, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating. Despite the positive analyst sentiment, insider transactions show recent net selling, with CEO Charles Robbins and other insiders disposing of a significant number of shares.

feed.title: Ashland grants CEO long-term equity award to retain leadership
feed.summary: Ashland Inc. has granted its Chair and CEO, Guillermo Novo, a one-time equity award of $2 million in restricted stock units. This award, vesting on December 31, 2028, is designed to ensure his continued leadership and align executive incentives with long-term shareholder interests. Analysts currently have a "Hold" rating on ASH stock with a $66.00 price target.

feed.title: Hyve Solutions Announces Leadership Transition
feed.summary: Hyve Solutions, a subsidiary of TD SYNNEX, announced that Jerry Kagele has been appointed President, succeeding Steve Ichinaga. Ichinaga, who founded Hyve Solutions 15 years ago, will transition to an advisory role for the next year. This leadership change is a planned move to ensure continued growth and operational continuity for the company.

feed.title: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Shares Up 7% Following Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) shares rose 7% after Evercore ISI increased its price target to $241, maintaining an "outperform" rating. The stock traded as high as $239.50, closing at $238.46 on Tuesday. Several other firms also hold "buy" or "strong buy" ratings, with a consensus price target of $202.81.

feed.title: Ascent Solar raises $10 million in private placement of shares
feed.summary: Ascent Solar Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASTI) has successfully closed a private placement, raising $10 million in gross proceeds through the sale of common stock and warrants. This capital infusion is crucial for the company, which develops thin-film solar panels, given its current market capitalization and cash burn rate. The warrants, if fully exercised, could generate an additional $15 million for general working capital needs.
